channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Tehran and Washington Face a ‘Wake Up Moment’ Over the War
item.description: U.S. and Iranian attacks are unfolding in a way that risks a wider war they cannot contain, analysts warn, unless they can return to negotiations quickly.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians, like these people walking under a poster of the country’s three supreme leaders, are growing increasingly frustrated with the war.

item.title: Five of the Main Challenges Awaiting Andy Burnham When He Becomes Prime Minister
item.description: Andy Burnham, who is set to enter Downing Street on Monday, faces a long and daunting list of issues. Here are some of the most urgent.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: The Labour Party’s Andy Burnham during an event in June in Manchester, England.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Another U.S. Service Member Is Killed as War Expands
item.description: The Pentagon said a service member was killed in northern Iraq while troops disposed of an Iranian drone. It also said “unidentified remains” were found in Jordan, where an earlier attack had left two soldiers dead and another missing.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: A satellite image of the Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, in Azraq, Jordan, which came under Iranian fire last week.

item.title: Why Tokyo’s New Shorts Policy is Sparking Debate in Japan
item.description: The Tokyo government is allowing workers to wear shorts for the first time this summer, prompting delicate questions about etiquette and hairy legs.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno
item.media:credit: Noriko Hayashi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Two officials wearing shorts at the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building, on Tuesday.

item.title: India’s CJP Party Leads Protest March to Parliament After Exam Leaks
item.description: The demonstrations were prompted in part after questions for the country’s biggest medical school entrance exam were leaked, forcing millions to retake the test.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Pragati K.B.
item.media:description: Police confronting people gathered ahead of a planned march by supporters of India’s Cockroach Janta Party toward Parliament in New Delhi on the opening day of its monsoon session on Monday.

item.title: Russia Batters Kyiv in One of the Largest Missile Assaults of the War
item.description: On Saturday, Ukraine hit two warehouses in Russia in strikes that killed at least eight people and injured 62.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd and Marc Santora
item.media:description: A helicopter dropping water on Sunday over the site of a Russian missile strike in the Kyiv region of Ukraine.

item.title: The U.S. Braced For the World Cup. It Fell in Love Instead.
item.description: Amid anxiety over how the United States would welcome the world, cities like Lawrence, Kan., and Boston threw open their doors. The bonds forged are still visible on the streets.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Fans celebrating during a match between France and Senegal at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J., in June.

item.title: A Modest Proposal for the Strait Dispute: A Shared Pact Among Gulf States
item.description: A London-based research group has invoked a now-defunct European pact on coal and steel to suggest forming a coalition of countries that would share control of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo vessels were anchored last month off the coast of Oman during an effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Wildfires Continue To Burn in Ontario, Canada, Sending Smoke to U.S. Cities
item.description: Nearly 200 fires continued to devastate swaths of the province as smoke continued to drift across parts of Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: Premier Doug Ford of Ontario after a news conference in Thunder Bay on Saturday. Roughly 1.7 million acres have burned in Ontario, data from the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Center shows.

item.title: Uproar Over Surrogate Baby Prompts German Official’s Sudden Resignation
item.description: Jens Spahn, a top leader of Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s party, resigned his post after announcing that he had a baby by surrogate, which is illegal in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.media:description: Jens Spahn, parliamentary group leader of Germany’s Christian Democratic Union party, in the plenary hall during a session at the Bundestag, Germany’s lower house of Parliament, in December.

item.title: Iqaluit, Canada’s Most Northern City, Gets Its First Bus Line
item.description: Jacinto Marques left Angola for Canada’s frozen edge. Now he’s running a bus service in Iqaluit, the largest, and only city in Canada’s Arctic.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi and Renaud Philippe
item.media:description: Looking out the window of an Iqaluit Transit bus during its route through the city.

item.title: A Very British CPAC: Few, if Any, Costumes and Not Much Trump
item.description: London hosted an understated version of the Conservative Political Action Conference, known in the United States for its over-the-top celebration of MAGA causes.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Matt Schlapp, chairman of the Conservative Political Action Conference, and the former prime minister Liz Truss onstage at CPAC’s London conference on Thursday.

item.title: Rubio Says That U.S. Is Still Open to Diplomacy With Iran
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio also said that Iran has signaled it wants to negotiate with the United States, even as the two sides escalated their attacks.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Brendan Smialowski/Agence France-Presse, Pool/AFP via Getty Images
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland, on Sunday night.

item.title: Tears in Argentina for Lionel Messi’s ‘Last Tango’
item.description: There were still fireworks and smoke bombs, but the nation was disappointed for its soccer star after he lost in his likely last World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola
item.media:credit: Sebastián López Brach for The New York Times
item.media:description: A watch party in Rosario, Argentina, Lionel Messi’s hometown, turned somber.

item.title: Did This World Cup Change Soccer Forever?
item.description: With a wartime host nation, a referee deported and a ban overturned after a call from the White House, the 2026 tournament tested FIFA like no other.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:credit: Joseph Fox for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fans watching the World Cup final in Madrid on Sunday. Spain won 1-0.

item.title: A Conversation with Zohran Mamdani
item.description: The mayor of New York City talks about Andy Burnham, Netanyahu and the controversial U.S. red card.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: Iran shipped billions in oil during the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Stacy Cowley
item.media:description: Iran capitalized on its export of oil, gaining billions of dollars during a short-lived peace agreement with the United States.

item.title: Earthquake in Peru Kills 6 and Displaces Hundreds
item.description: Peru is prone to frequent quakes, but the 5.5-magnitude temblor was the biggest to strike the central highlands area in more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Mitra Taj
item.media:description: Una casa en Chupuro, Perú, que fue destruida por el terremoto el sábado.

item.title: Israel watches tensely as the U.S.-Iran fighting escalates.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to continue the military campaign against Iran.

item.title: The U.S. Is Sending More Warplanes to the Middle East, Officials Say
item.description: F-16 and F-35 jets were en route to the region from Europe, according to U.S. officials who were not authorized to speak publicly.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Fighter jets stationed in Germany and Britain are being deployed to the Middle East according to officials.

item.title: Increased Cooperation With U.S. Has Put Jordan in Iran’s Cross Hairs
item.description: Jordan’s role in U.S. operations against Iran has grown as other allies in the region have restricted Washington’s ability to use their bases and airspace.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Iran’s recent attacks on U.S.-allied Jordan, including one that killed two U.S. servicemen on Friday, introduce a new complexity in the war.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Yan Zhuang, Hari Raj and Eric Schmitt

item.title: Argentines, Already ‘Losing Their Minds,’ Brace for World Cup Final
item.description: A championship victory would be pure elation, but Argentina is already basking in a state of grace after a win against England and a monthlong fever dream.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola, Natalie Alcoba, Lucía Cholakian Herrera and Daniel Politi
item.media:credit: Sebastián López Brach for The New York Times
item.media:description: Thousands of people celebrated Argentina’s victory over England, and its qualification for the World Cup final, on Wednesday in Rosario, Argentina.

item.title: Why Jordan Is Becoming a New Focus in the U.S.-Iran War
item.description: Four Iranian attacks in five days have targeted U.S. troops in Jordan, U.S. officials said, including one on Friday that killed two soldiers and left another service member missing.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Julian E. Barnes and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: American and Jordanian forces took part in multinational military exercises hosted by Jordan in 2022.

item.title: Canada Wildfires: What to Know
item.description: Hundreds of wildfires in Canada and the United States have blanketed large sections of North America in dense smoke over the past week.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
item.media:description: The observation deck of Toronto’s CN Tower on Wednesday, as wildfire smoke enveloped downtown.

item.title: Deaths of 2 U.S. Troops in Jordan Brings American Toll in the War With Iran to 16
item.description: President Trump vowed to avenge the deaths of any U.S. military personnel.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The remains of a U.S. soldier killed in Kuwait earlier in the war, arrived at Dover Air Force Base, Del., in March.

item.title: Ukraine’s Top General Becomes a Target of Protesters’ Anger
item.description: Demonstrators supporting Ukraine’s ousted defense minister have begun denouncing the commander he clashed with in office, Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer and Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: People in Kyiv, Ukraine, on Thursday protesting President Volodymyr Zelensky’s decision to oust Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who had championed drone warfare.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Leave Iranians Isolated and Scared
item.description: Overnight strikes damaged a tunnel and three bridges in Hormozgan Province, the governor’s office said.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Persian Gulf off the coast of Bandar Abbas, an Iranian port city in the south of the country, in May. Residents said roads around the city were heavily damaged by U.S. airstrikes this week.

item.title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live
item.description: Everyone is watching the goals after they happen, and some get them significantly faster.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: Lautaro Martinez of Argentina scored the winning goal over England on Wednesday. When did you see it?

item.title: Kuwait accuses Iran of targeting civilian sites after attacks spark fires.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Most Forest Fires in Canada Are Simply Impossible to Put Out
item.description: Half of Canada’s wildfires are in remote areas with no road access. Impossible to fight, they are often left to burn.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: A wildfire burning in Bison Lake, Alberta, last year.

item.title: In Bangladesh, Argentina’s Soccer Team Finds a Huge Fan Base
item.description: Bangladesh has never qualified for the World Cup. But every four years, many in the country adopt Argentina’s team as their own.
item.dc:creator: Saif Hasnat
item.media:credit: Munir Uz Zaman/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Fans of Argentina’s soccer team gathering last month in Dhaka, Bangladesh, during the World Cup.

item.title: Freedom Convoy Leader, Under House Arrest, Wants to Visit the White House
item.description: Prosecutors say Tamara Lich wants a “blank cheque” for international travel, including a cruise, arguing that she is trying to profit from the very protest that got her sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: The Freedom Convoy organizer Tamara Lich at her sentencing hearing last summer. She is asking a court to let her travel to the United States.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Aaron Boxerman, Leily Nikounazar and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Lamine Yamal, Soccer Star and Son of African Migrants, Personifies a Changing Spain
item.description: As he prepares for the World Cup final, Lamine Yamal, 19, is at the heart of a national debate about what it means to be Spanish in an increasingly multicultural society.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:credit: Maria Contreras Coll for The New York Times
item.media:description: A crowd near Rocafonda, the neighborhood where Lamine Yamal grew up, watches the player on a giant screen during Spain’s semifinal with France last week.

item.title: Russia Bill Broadens Sanctions but Could Fuel Fears Over the Dollar
item.description: The Trump administration has recently sought to scale back sanctions as adversaries develop workarounds that some believe could erode the U.S. dollar’s supremacy.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of Foreign Assets Control (United States Treasury)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Treasury Department in Washington.

item.title: Israel Counters Iranian Spying by Warning Against Recruitment
item.description: The Israeli authorities have enacted a broad array of countermeasures to contain what they describe as online recruiting efforts by Iranian agents.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: People walking in Tehran this month. According to Israeli authorities, Iran has intensified efforts to recruit people in Israel for intelligence gathering.

item.title: Indian Activist on Hunger Strike Is Forcibly Removed by Police
item.description: The activist, Sonam Wangchuk, has been demanding the education minister’s resignation over botched nationwide exams that affected millions of students.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal

item.title: As Trump Scraps With Meloni, His Envoy to Italy Is at Sea
item.description: Tilman J. Fertitta, the U.S. ambassador to Rome, is summering on his superyacht as President Trump squabbles with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Gianni Cipriano
item.media:description: Tilman Fertitta aboard his yacht, Boardwalk, on the Fourth of July.

item.title: They Called Sam Neill ‘Skux.’ (It Was a Compliment.)
item.description: The New Zealand slang word, dating back to the 1990s, was pulled back into circulation in tributes to the actor, who died on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.media:description: Sam Neill at the Toronto International Film Festival in 2019.

item.title: 7 Americans Sent to Disputed Kenya Ebola Site After New Trump Travel Ban
item.description: The aid workers were on the frontline in the fight against the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and have shown no symptoms, their employer said.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A satellite image showing the field hospital at Laikipia Air Base in central Kenya, where America aid workers are being quarantined.

item.title: The Rise and Fall of Ukraine’s Drone Warfare Mastermind
item.description: The popularity of drones, and the defense minister who heavily promoted them, helped lead to a clash in which President Volodymyr Zelensky sided with the military brass.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov, the former minister of defense for Ukraine, speaking Thursday in Kyiv. Mr. Fedorov’s growing reliance on drones had reportedly irked some members of the military’s leadership.

item.title: Karim Khan’s Accuser Breaks Silence on Alleged Sexual Misconduct at I.C.C.
item.description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor at the International Criminal Court, has denied the accusations. He faces a vote next week that will determine whether he keeps his job.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, at a U.N. Security Council meeting.

item.title: War Between U.S. and Iran Expands, With Strikes Across the Region
item.description: Videos and reports in Iranian state media showed damage to bridges, railways and other infrastructure. U.S. allies in the region reported retaliatory strikes by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar, Shirin Hakim and Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An image taken from social media and verified by The New York Times showing a damaged portion of a bridge in Hormozgan Province, Iran, on Friday.

item.title: Lam Wing-Kee, Hong Kong Bookseller Who Defied Authorities, Dies at 70
item.description: One of five booksellers kidnapped by Chinese officials in 2015, Mr. Lam spoke out after his release and became an international cause célèbre.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Causeway Bay Books (Causeway Bay, Hong Kong, Retailer)
item.media:credit: An Rong Xu for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lam Wing Kee in 2020 at the Causeway Bay Bookstore in Taipei, Taiwan. Taiwan’s ministry of culture saluted the store as “an inspiration and role model for democracy, liberty and human rights across borders.”

item.title: Prominent Cuban Artist Will Go Into Exile After 5-Year Prison Term
item.description: The United States offered a visa to Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, who was expected to fly to Miami on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara in Havana in 2021.

item.title: Israeli Strike in Gaza Kills Seven People, Health Officials Say
item.description: The Israeli military said the attack targeted members of the second-biggest militant group in Gaza. It also said it was “aware of the claims that several uninvolved individuals” had been harmed.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.media:description: Palestinians carrying the body of a man killed during an Israeli strike in central Gaza on Friday.

item.title: Burnham Becomes Labour Leader and Britain’s Incoming Prime Minister
item.description: At a time of political upheaval and economic stagnation, Andy Burnham will on Monday become the seventh prime minister in a decade.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Andy Burnham, the new leader of the Labour Party, speaking in London on Friday.

item.title: 7.3-Magnitude Earthquake Off Coast of Mexico and Guatemala Poses Tsunami Threat
item.description: A powerful earthquake near the border of Mexico and Guatemala raised fears of a tsunami and shook buildings across three countries.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.media:description: People outside the Biblioteca Nacional in San Salvador, El Salvador, following a 7.4 magnitude earthquake on Friday.

item.title: Parents in Uganda Demand Answers After Bus Crash Kills 21 Children
item.description: The driver of the school bus, carrying dozens of students, lost control, officials said, causing the bus to veer off the road into a boulder.
item.dc:creator: Musinguzi Blanshe
item.media:description: An overturned school bus in eastern Uganda, where 21 students and five adults were killed in the crash.

item.title: E.U. Proposes Changes to Emissions Trading System
item.description: The European Union is facing a green backlash as emissions cuts become harder and require bigger overhauls to industry and livelihoods
item.dc:creator: Chico Harlan
item.category: Carbon Caps and Emissions Trading Programs
item.media:description: Europe, which often holds itself up as a climate leader, has been facing political infighting over some of its policies.

item.title: Trump Faces the Limits of U.S. Firepower and the Lessons of Past Wars
item.description: Like his predecessors, President Trump has struggled to turn battlefield successes into long-term victories.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We’re going to knock out all their power plants,” President Trump told Fox News this week. “We’re going to knock out all their bridges unless they get to the table and negotiate.”

item.title: Cuban Dissident Was Missing A Week After Prison Release
item.description: A Cuban artist awaiting permission from the Trump administration to enter the United States was held in an unknown location in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara in 2018.

item.title: Iran-U.S. Talks: How 4 Negotiators Would Approach Diplomacy
item.description: First step: Agree on a goal.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran in June. The likelihood of an easing of tension between the United States and Iran looks distant at the moment, but there are small signals that diplomacy could still be possible.
